steps.title: Find the yearly savings
steps.content: The old yearly energy cost is \$4,820 and the new yearly energy cost is \$3,140, so the yearly savings are $$4{,}820-3{,}140.$$

steps.title: Represent savings after t years
steps.content: After $t$ years, the cumulative savings are $$(4{,}820-3{,}140)t.$$ The savings must exceed the \$28,000 installation cost, so the savings must be greater than $28{,}000$.
